howToPlay.seoDescription: Learn how MogulGame works: browse real properties, place pretend offers, and win when properties sell.
howToPlay.title: How to Play
howToPlay.overview: MogulGame is a real estate simulation game where you compete against other players by placing pretend offers on real properties listed on Zillow and StreetEasy. Start with $1,000,000 in pretend USD and see how far your real estate instincts can take you.
howToPlay.step1Desc: Create a free account and receive $1,000,000 in pretend USD. This is your starting capital to make offers on real properties.
howToPlay.step2Desc: Search properties currently listed for sale on Zillow and StreetEasy. View photos, prices, and neighborhood details on the map.
howToPlay.step3Desc: Found a property you like? Place a pretend offer. You can offer up to 5x your current balance. The goal is to bid as low as possible while still beating the eventual sale price.
howToPlay.step4Desc: When the property sells in real life, all pretend offers are automatically evaluated against the actual closing price.
howToPlay.step5Desc: If your offer qualifies and is the lowest qualifying bid, you win! Earn pretend USD from competing bidders or a solo bonus.
howToPlay.winningRule1: Only offers placed before the real closing date are considered.
howToPlay.winningRule2: Only offers above the actual closing price qualify. You must bid higher than what the property actually sold for.
howToPlay.winningRule3: The lowest qualifying offer wins. Smart moguls find the sweet spot — just above the sale price.
howToPlay.payoutRule1: If there are multiple qualifying offers, the highest losing bidder pays the difference between their offer and the winner's offer to the winner.
howToPlay.payoutRule2: If the winner is the only qualifier, they receive a 5% solo bonus on their offer amount.
howToPlay.exampleText: A property sells for $1,000,000. Three players bid: $1,010,000 (Player A), $1,030,000 (Player B), and $1,150,000 (Player C). Player A wins with the lowest qualifying bid. Player C (highest loser) pays $1,150,000 - $1,010,000 = $140,000 to Player A.
howToPlay.whyPlayPrompt: Wondering why practicing real estate pricing matters?
howToPlay.whyPlayLink: Learn why MogulGame can help you

whyPlay.seoTitle: Why Play MogulGame - Practice Real Estate Pricing Risk-Free | Sudobility
whyPlay.seoDescription: Build confidence in real estate pricing before making the biggest purchase of your life. MogulGame is paper trading for real estate — practice offers on real properties risk-free.
whyPlay.heroSubtitle: A home is the biggest purchase most people ever make. MogulGame lets you practice pricing real properties risk-free — so when it's time to make a real offer, you'll know exactly what to bid.
whyPlay.stakesP1: In the stock market, you can start investing with as little as $1. In real estate, the minimum commitment is hundreds of thousands of dollars. There's no room for trial and error when the stakes are this high.
whyPlay.stakesP2: First-time buyers made up just 24% of home purchases in recent years, with the median age rising to 38. Many people delay buying simply because they don't feel confident enough to make an offer — they're unsure what a property is actually worth.
whyPlay.stakesP3: MogulGame changes that. By practicing with real listings and real closing prices, you develop the pricing intuition that takes seasoned investors years to build.
whyPlay.paperTradingP1: On Wall Street, every new trader starts with a simulator. Platforms like Webull give you $1,000,000 in virtual cash to practice trading stocks, options, and futures — risk-free. It's how millions of investors build confidence before putting in real money.
whyPlay.paperTradingP2: MogulGame applies the same proven concept to real estate. You start with $1,000,000 in pretend USD and place offers on real properties listed for sale across the United States. When those properties sell in real life, your offers are evaluated against the actual closing price.
whyPlay.paperTradingP3: But unlike the stock market — where a bad trade might cost you $50 — a bad real estate decision can cost tens of thousands. That's why practicing with MogulGame is even more valuable.
whyPlay.realLearningP1: Every property on MogulGame is a real listing from the US market. You can see actual photos, prices, neighborhood details, and property history — just like you would on Zillow or Realtor.com.
whyPlay.realLearningP2: We encourage players to go beyond the screen: visit open houses, walk the neighborhoods, talk to real estate agents. Understanding a property's true value means seeing it in person — the noise levels, natural light, finishes, and neighborhood feel that photos can't capture.
whyPlay.realLearningP3: When you combine real-world research with MogulGame's pricing feedback, you learn faster than any textbook or course could teach you. You'll know within weeks whether your pricing instincts are accurate — or need calibration.
whyPlay.instinctsP1: What makes one home sell for $50,000 above asking while another sits on the market for months? Pricing is driven by location, comparable sales, property condition, and market timing. MogulGame teaches you to read these signals.
whyPlay.instinctsP2: The game rewards precision: the lowest offer above the closing price wins. This trains you to find the sweet spot — close enough to the real value that you'd beat the actual buyer, but not so high that you overpay.
whyPlay.instinctsP3: Over time, you'll develop a feel for what properties are actually worth versus what they're listed for. That confidence is invaluable when you're ready to make your first — or next — real offer.
